Know.description: The doer ought to know the fact or understand the fact clearly.

specific_fields.subject: The person who knows or ought to know the fact
specific_fields.known_fact: The specific fact or situation understood

Surrendering.description: The doer voluntarily surrendering after committing a crime.

Confession.description: The suspect or defendant telling the facts to the police.

specific_fields.confessor: The suspect or defendant making the statement

Understanding.description: The forgiveness from the victim or victim's families to the criminal.

specific_fields.forgiver: The victim or family member granting forgiveness
specific_fields.reason_or_condition: Why or under what terms forgiveness was granted

Compensation.description: The act of compensating the victim for his loss, damage, or injury.

specific_fields.compensation_amount: The specific amount of money or items given

Return_stolen_goods.description: The act of returning the stolen money or stolen goods to the victim or government.

specific_fields.receiver: The victim or authority receiving the items
specific_fields.returned_items: The specific goods or amount of money returned

Disposal_of_stolen_goods.description: The act of destroying stolen goods, selling stolen goods, or squandering stolen money.

specific_fields.actor: The person disposing of the goods
specific_fields.disposed_items: The stolen goods or money involved
specific_fields.disposal_method: How it was disposed of (e.g., sold, destroyed, spent)

Dividing_stolen_goods.description: The act of sharing stolen goods or money.

specific_fields.divided_items: The specific goods or money split

Search_Seizure.description: The search and inspection of the suspect's body, articles, residence, or the seizure of contraband.

specific_fields.law_enforcement: The officers or agency conducting the search
specific_fields.target: The suspect, residence, or area searched

Reporting.description: The act of reporting bad people or bad things to relevant units.

specific_fields.reported_party: The person or entity being reported
specific_fields.reported_facts: The misconduct or incident being reported

Arrest.description: The act of detaining or arresting suspects.

Reporting_to_police.description: The act of calling the police to ask for help or reporting a case to the police.

Identifying.description: The investigation organ appoints or hires people with expertise to make a scientific judgment and draw professional conclusions.

specific_fields.identified_object: What is being tested or examined (e.g., DNA, injury)

Traffic_accident.description: A traffic accident happens, which usually causes personal injury, death or property loss.

specific_fields.vehicles: The specific cars or transport involved

Fire_accident.description: The disaster caused by uncontrolled combustion.

specific_fields.damage_or_casualties: What was burned or who was hurt

Explosion_accident.description: The disaster caused by a sudden release of a large amount of energy, leading to property losses and casualties.

Flood_and_waterlogging.description: A large amount of water covers an area that is usually dry.

specific_fields.severity: Depth of water or extent of damage

Drought.description: Little or no rain during a long period of time.

specific_fields.duration: How long the drought has lasted

Landslides.description: A geographic disaster caused by a mass of earth or rock falling down the slope of a mountain.

Death.description: The state of a human being dead.

specific_fields.body_part: Which part of the body was damaged

Being_trapped.description: The state in which people are physically in trouble and can't get out.

Being_poisoned.description: One's discomfort caused by toxic effects, emphasizing the state of one's being poisoned.

specific_fields.estimated_value: The monetary worth of the loss

Damage.description: The fact that the property has been damaged.

specific_fields.damaged_property: The specific item or real estate harmed
specific_fields.extent_of_damage: How badly it was broken or damaged

Verbal_conflict.description: Oral conflicts happen between two or more people without physical contact.

Physical_conflict.description: A physical clash that happens between two or more people, including fighting. Emphasizes mutual behavior.

specific_fields.weapons_or_tools: Any items used in the fight

Selling.description: One's act of selling something for a profit.

Buying.description: One's act of buying or consuming something.

Leasing_Lending.description: The act of renting or lending something to others.

specific_fields.borrower: The person receiving the item temporarily
specific_fields.item_leased: The property, vehicle, or item lent

Renting_Borrowing.description: The act of renting or borrowing something from others.

specific_fields.borrower: The person taking the item temporarily

Return_Repayment.description: The act of returning something to its original place or owner.

Gaining_profits.description: One obtaining money or other benefits through a certain act or activity.

Employing.description: The act of giving others a job to do for payment.

Lending_money.description: Specialized institutions or people making loans to earn profits, including bank loans and individual loans.

specific_fields.lender: The bank or individual providing funds

Raising_money.description: The act of raising money from unspecified majority people.

specific_fields.fundraiser: The individual or group collecting funds

Payment_Delivery.description: The act of giving money or other things to others.

Entering_into_contract_agreement.description: Two or more person/groups signing contracts, including written contracts, oral agreements, etc.

Manufacturing.description: Producing, manufacturing, or making tangible objects, emphasizing from scratch.

specific_fields.manufacturer: The person or entity making the object

Desertion.description: One's act of actively abandoning or discarding something or someone.

specific_fields.abandoned_target: The person, animal, or item left behind

Transport.description: One's act of transporting someone or something from one place to another.

specific_fields.transported_target: What or who is being moved

Mailing.description: Delivering documents or articles through the post office or third-party postal service.

Dispersal.description: The act of spreading information, data, rumors to the unspecified majority of people on the Internet or in public.

specific_fields.platform: Where it was spread (e.g., internet forum)

Communication.description: The connection between two or more people, such as making a phone call.

Informing.description: One's act of telling others information or reminding others of certain information.

Introducing.description: One's behavior to make other people or groups know each other or have a connection.

Inviting_Recruiting.description: The acts of recruiting, inviting others to a place, or inviting others to do something or participate in an activity.

specific_fields.purpose: What they are being asked to do

Gathering.description: The act of gathering a group of people together.

Preventing_Nuisance.description: One's act of preventing things from going smoothly or hindering others from doing something by words or actions.

specific_fields.prevented_action: What they were stopped from doing

Provocation.description: One attempting to trigger off conflicts with others, or trigger off conflicts between other two groups.

Helping_Rescuing.description: One's act of helping others to do something, saving, rescuing, or assisting others who are injured or in trouble.

specific_fields.assistance_provided: What specifically was done to help

Supply.description: One providing materials, conditions, intelligence information, or other specific things to others.

Indulging.description: One's act of allowing bad things to develop without any interference.

specific_fields.indulger: The person turning a blind eye
specific_fields.indulged_party: The person doing the bad thing
specific_fields.ignored_action: The bad behavior allowed to happen

Tracking.description: One's act of following others quietly without being detected.

Consenting_Accepting.description: One agreeing with the opinions of others, accepting others' asks, or accepting the property given by others.

specific_fields.accepted_item_or_proposal: What they agreed to or took

Reject_Against.description: One rejecting others' asks or the property given by others.

Terminate_Waiver.description: One stopping doing something, giving up the original persistence, or giving up a right.

specific_fields.waived_right_or_action: What was given up or halted

Request.description: One putting forward specific matters or wishes, hoping or requiring others to realize them.

specific_fields.requested_party: Who is expected to fulfill it

Suggesting.description: One putting forward a plan or idea to others.

Make_appointment.description: The act of two or more people discussing and determining something.

Drink.description: One's act of drinking alcohol, usually accompanied by other behaviors, such as driving, etc.

Killing.description: One's act of killing others in order to make others die.

Bodily_harm.description: The act of harming the physical health of others, usually manifested in beating.

Verbal_abuse.description: The act of insulting, attacking or hurting others through language.

Blackmail.description: The act of demanding money from others by threatening or deceiving them.

Threatening_Forcing.description: The act of forcing others to do or not do something through violence or power.

specific_fields.forced_action: What they were made to do or stop doing

Bearing_arms.description: One's holding or carrying sticks, props, guns, or other instruments.

Detention_restriction.description: The act of depriving or restricting the freedom of others, such as binding or detaining people in specific places.

Kidnapping.description: The act of taking hostages by violent means in exchange for interests, emphasizing that the object must be people.

specific_fields.demands: What the kidnapper wants in return

Defraud.description: The act of covering up the real situation with false words or actions to deceive others.

Abducting.description: One's act of cheating someone away by luring, cheating, or other means.

Impersonating.description: The act of disguising a real thing with a false thing or one's act of pretending to be somebody in order to trick people.

specific_fields.impersonated_identity: Who or what they pretended to be

Falsifying.description: The act of making fake goods or false news.

specific_fields.falsified_object: The forged document, good, or news

Altering.description: The act of modifying real basis A without authorization to make it have another illusion B.

Theft.description: One's act of stealing others' property by secret.

Plunder.description: One's act of seizing property blatantly in front of the victims and taking them away (without deadly force/weapons).

Robbery.description: One's act of using violent means to rob others' property, such as robbery with a knife.

Misappropriation.description: The act of changing the original use of the property to another without authorization.

specific_fields.unauthorized_use: What it was actually spent on

Embezzlement.description: One's act of taking others' property illegally, including real estate, emphasizing the state of possession.

specific_fields.embezzler: The person entrusted who stole it
specific_fields.victim_organization: The company or entity stolen from

Destroying.description: One's act of destroying property, this event has a subject.

Indecency.description: One's act of forcibly sexually harassing others by touching private parts or other acts other than adultery.

Rape.description: One's act of forcing women to have sex when they do not want to.

specific_fields.method: Use of force, drugs, or coercion

Prostitution.description: Women providing paid sexual services to others.

Whoring.description: One purchasing sexual service with money.

Opening_casinos.description: One's act of opening casinos for multiple plays to gamble on.

specific_fields.scale: Size or profit of the operation

Direct_Encourage.description: One's act of letting others commit crimes by means of command, inspiration, or temptation.

specific_fields.instigated_crime: What crime they were pushed to commit

Collusion.description: The act of two or more people scheming a crime plan together.

specific_fields.planned_crime: What they are planning to do

Illegal_driving.description: One's act of driving a car illegally.

specific_fields.violation_type: e.g., drunk driving, without a license

Disclosure_information.description: One's act of disclosing information that should be kept secret.

Concealing.description: One's act of hiding something from discovery.

specific_fields.concealed_item: What was hidden (e.g., weapons, evidence, people)

Home_invasion.description: One's act of invading or sneaking into other people's private space without the permission of others.

Bribery.description: One's act of bribing others with property to seek illegitimate interests or accepting others' property to seek illegitimate interests for others.

Escaping.description: An escaping event means one's escaping and hiding in order to avoid capture.

Arson.description: One's act of setting on fire.

Smuggling.description: A smuggling event refers to the act of one's illegally transporting goods into or out of the country in violation of customs regulations.

Poisoning.description: A poisoning event refers to one putting poison in containers or a specific environment in order to kill people, animals, or plants.

Suicide.description: A suicide event refers to the act of one's killing himself.
